rulings.Case Title: DISTRICT COUNCIL OF IRON WORKERS VS. SOUTH COAST STEEL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 24, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER DISTRICT COUNCIL OF IRON WORKERS' MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. Petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. A petition to confirm arbitration award may be filed within 4 years after the service of the Arbitration Award. (CCP § 1288.) However, a motion to vacate or correct an award must be filed within 100 days after service of the award. (Id.) Further, CCP §1288.2 provides that "[a] response requesting that an award be vacated or that an award be corrected shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of service of a signed copy of the award upon" the respondent. The 100 day time period is jurisdictional, and the court has no power to extend that mandatory deadline. (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211.) Here, the award was served on Respondent on or about March 2, 2015. (See Declaration of James Husebo, Pg. 3.) The time to have the award corrected or vacated therefore expired on June 10, 2015, 100 days after March 2, 2015. Thus, Respondent's opposition to confirm arbitration and request that the court vacate the award, filed on July 13, 2015, is untimely. Request for attorneys' fees is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under C.C.P 436
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 24, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion To Strike Under C.C.P 436. The motion is denied. Plaintiff relies on an inapposite appellate doctrine. "An appellate court has the inherent power, under the 'disentitlement doctrine,' to dismiss an appeal by a party that refuses to comply with a lower court order." Stoltenberg v. Ampton Investments (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1225, 1229. There is no basis to strike the general denial under CCP 435 and 436. If defendant fails to comply with discovery in this case, then plaintiff may pursue its remedies under the California Civil Discovery Act. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. CHOW et al VS. TED WIN WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 24, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS CHARLES CHOW, YIU CHEUNG, CHUNG KWAN, AND SUE HING BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Reopen Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Since plaintiff filed no reply, it is unclear why there is a need for the additional 5 depositions, assuming that the four continued depositions are going forward. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUBA TROYANOVSKY VS. IRINA KAZIMIRSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) To Quash Defendants Designation Of Expert Witnesses Dated June 11, 2015, (2) To Exclude At Trial The Testimony Of Clement Jones, M.D. And Peter Chase, D.D.S., Or (3) For A Protective Order Limiting The Fee Defendants Experts May Charge For Giving Deposition Testimony
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 24, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LUBA TROYANOVSKY'S Motion (1) To Quash Defendants Designation Of Expert Witnesses Dated June 11, 2015, (2) To Exclude At Trial The Testimony Of Clement Jones, M.D. And Peter Chase, D.D.S., Or (3) For A Protective Order Limiting The Fee Defendants Experts May Charge For Giving Deposition Testimony. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. There appears to be a dispute as to the meaning of the stipulation with respect to expert disclosure, but there does not appear to have been a subsequent request for disclosure of experts served?. Assuming that a new expert disclosure was triggered by the new trial date, are these new experts duplicative of the old experts pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 2034.250(b)(6) and are their fees excessive (see Marsh v. Mountain Zephyr Inc. (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 289, 304). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff Warren Legaries Deposition Subpoena To Records Custodian Of Irate Iguana, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff Warren Legarie's Deposition Subpoena To Records Custodian Of Irate Iguana, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The complaint alleges a conspiracy between Plaintiff's former partner and the landlord to transfer corporate assets and that the former partner is involved in the new restaurant. Parties should address each of the categories of documents under the relevant to the subject matter discovery test and how they are balanced against privacy issues, whether defendant landlord has custody or control of the records, and the issue of the need for a protective order to the extent that any documents are to be produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)
